item.title: Universal gates from braiding and fusing anyons on quantum hardware
item.author: Chiu Fan Bowen LoAnasuya LyonsMohsin Iqbal
item.content:encoded:
A 54-qubit realization of non-Abelian S3 topological order demonstrates that combining anyon braiding with fusion enables universal topological quantum computation.

Chiu Fan Bowen LoAnasuya LyonsMohsin Iqbal

Nature

item.title: All-mechanical coherence protection and fast control of a spin qubit
item.author: Eliza CornellZhujing XuMarko Lončar
item.content:encoded:
Silicon–vacancy spins can be used in phononic quantum devices, but improvements in coherence times are still required. It has now been shown that the spin coherence can be protected by continuous driving using acoustic waves.

Eliza CornellZhujing XuMarko Lončar

Nature Physics

item.title: Quantum statistical plasmonic metacrystals
item.author: Chenglong YouRiley B. DawkinsOmar S. Magaña-Loaiza
item.content:encoded:
A new class of materials, quantum statistical plasmonic metacrystals, is described, which can selectively transmit different types of light based only on their quantum coherence, showing bands of allowed and forbidden photon statistics.

Chenglong YouRiley B. DawkinsOmar S. Magaña-Loaiza

Nature

item.title: Optical filter sorts light by its ‘quantum statistics’
item.author: Sebastian Golat
item.content:encoded:
A nanostructured gold surface can filter light depending on the tendency of its photons to arrive at a detector in pairs.

Sebastian Golat

Nature

item.title: Catalyst-free, microdroplet-mediated waste plastic conversion to diacids
item.author: Ruiliang GaoLiwei ZhangYong Wang
item.content:encoded:
A catalyst-free upcycling strategy based on in situ generation of hydroxyl radicals at microdroplet interfaces is described for efficient and scalable processing of polymers with minimal infrastructure, using only water and oxygen under mild conditions.

Ruiliang GaoLiwei ZhangYong Wang

Nature

item.title: Ammonia pressure controls colloidal metal nitride synthesis in molten salts
item.author: Ruiming LinVikash KhokharDmitri V. Talapin
item.content:encoded:
A molten salt, high-pressure synthesis method enables solution-based production of diverse refractory metal nitride nanocrystals, expanding access to technologically important nitride materials for advanced applications.

Ruiming LinVikash KhokharDmitri V. Talapin

Nature

item.title: Error correction of a logical qubit encoded in a single atomic ion
item.author: Kyle DeBryNadine MeisterJohn Chiaverini
item.content:encoded:
Quantum error correction typically encodes one logical qubit across many physical qubits to suppress noise. An experiment now encodes and corrects a qubit within the multiple internal states of a single trapped ion, reducing errors and extending the coherence time.

Kyle DeBryNadine MeisterJohn Chiaverini

Nature Physics

item.title: Emergence of phase coherence in a magnon Bose–Einstein condensate
item.author: Malte KosterMatthias R. SchweizerGeorg von Freymann
item.content:encoded:
Directly observing how a coherent global phase forms has long been challenging. Now an incoherent magnon gas is observed to transform into a state with a macroscopic global phase, confirming a key prediction of Bose–Einstein condensation theory.

Malte KosterMatthias R. SchweizerGeorg von Freymann

Nature Physics

item.title: Principles of optics in Fock space for the scalable manipulation of large quantum states
item.author: Yifang XuYilong ZhouLuyan Sun
item.content:encoded:
Bosonic quantum states can be understood as superpositions of eigenstates with fixed numbers of bosons. Now experiments show that mapping this representation to classical wave propagation offers an intuitive way to manipulate large quantum states.

Yifang XuYilong ZhouLuyan Sun

Nature Physics

item.title: Hybrid nanophotonic trap for cold atoms using surface forces and a blue-detuned evanescent field
item.author: Riccardo PennettaAntoine GlicensteinArno Rauschenbeutel
item.content:encoded:
A hybrid nanophotonic trap for cold caesium atoms uses surface forces for attraction and blue-detuned light for repulsion to achieve efficient loading and enhanced storage and coherence times.

Riccardo PennettaAntoine GlicensteinArno Rauschenbeutel

Nature Photonics

item.title: Millisecond coherence times in gigahertz-frequency mechanical oscillators
item.author: Yizhi LuoHilel Hagai DiamandiPeter T. Rakich
item.content:encoded:
Phonon decoherence in quantum high-overtone bulk acoustic-wave resonators is dominated by imperfections under the material surface. Removing the damaged subsurface layer substantially increases the performance of these devices.

Yizhi LuoHilel Hagai DiamandiPeter T. Rakich

Nature Physics

item.title: Bose–Einstein condensate of ultracold sodium–rubidium molecules with tunable dipolar interactions
item.author: Zhaopeng ShiZerong HuangDajun Wang
item.content:encoded:
Condensing ultracold polar molecules requires efficient cooling despite collisional losses. Now sodium–rubidium molecules have been brought to condensation and tuned into a self-bound droplet through control of their dipolar interactions.

Zhaopeng ShiZerong HuangDajun Wang

Nature Physics

item.title: Emergence of Fermi’s golden rule in a quantum many-body system
item.author: Jianyi ChenSongtao HuangNir Navon
item.content:encoded:
Spectroscopy of quantum many-body systems often relies on Fermi’s golden rule, but its regime of validity is unclear. Now measurements in a strongly interacting Fermi gas map the probe dynamics and identify where the rule holds and breaks down.

Jianyi ChenSongtao HuangNir Navon

Nature Physics

item.title: Reinforcement learning control of quantum error correction
item.author: Volodymyr SivakAlexis MorvanPaul V. Klimov
item.content:encoded:
By integrating reinforcement learning with quantum error correction, a quantum computer continuously self-calibrates during computation, achieving record logical error rates and enhanced resilience to drift.

Volodymyr SivakAlexis MorvanPaul V. Klimov

Nature

item.title: Publisher Correction: A 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum computer with all-to-all connectivity
item.author: Anthony RansfordM. S. AllmanJustin G. Bohnet
item.content:encoded:
Anthony RansfordM. S. AllmanJustin G. Bohnet

Nature

item.title: Visualization of the Zhang–Rice singlet, electronic molecules and Cooper pair formation in a cuprate superconductor
item.author: Shusen YeJianfa ZhaoYayu Wang
item.content:encoded:
How individual doped holes turn into superconducting Cooper pairs as doping increases in unconventional superconductors is still not well understood. Now, this process is observed in a paradigmatic cuprate compound.

Shusen YeJianfa ZhaoYayu Wang

Nature Physics

item.title: Topological textures in polar materials
item.author: Zijian HongYu-Tsun ShaoSujit Das
item.content:encoded:
Ferroelectric materials can host robust and diverse polarization textures. This Review examines their formation mechanisms, responses to external stimuli and potential applications in next-generation electronic devices.

Zijian HongYu-Tsun ShaoSujit Das

Nature Physics

item.title: When atoms tunnel as one
item.author: Simon Haine
item.content:encoded:
By making atoms tunnel together as a bound cluster, researchers have generated massive Schrödinger cat states, opening new routes toward tests of gravity in the quantum regime.

Simon Haine

Nature Physics

item.title: Altermagnetic spintronics
item.author: T. JungwirthJ. SinovaL. Šmejkal
item.content:encoded:
Altermagnetism, a recently identified unconventional magnetic order, might enable fast and efficient spin-based devices. This Review explores its interplay with ferroelectric and superconducting systems and its promise for scalable spintronics.

T. JungwirthJ. SinovaL. Šmejkal

Nature Physics

item.title: Fast thermalization with quantum algorithms
item.author: Anirban Chowdhury
item.content:encoded:
Quantum algorithms excel at simulating Hamiltonian dynamics, but they struggle with preparing the thermal equilibrium states needed for quantum simulation. Now it has been shown that a recent algorithm can efficiently prepare an important class of thermal states.

Anirban Chowdhury

Nature Physics

item.title: Organic cocrystals offer record photothermal conversion
item.author: Rong-Ran LiangXin Zhao
item.content:encoded:
Polar cocrystals made from two centrosymmetric components use quantum coherent beating to enhance photothermal conversion, setting a new high temperature for organic solids exposed to laser light.

Rong-Ran LiangXin Zhao

Nature Photonics

item.title: Can you actually do a nine-to-five PhD? Nature readers weigh in
item.author: Laura Woodrow
item.content:encoded:
Nature readers share their PhD time-management woes.

Laura Woodrow

Nature

item.title: Tangled up in spin
item.author: Meigan Aronson
item.content:encoded:
Neutron scattering measurements on a strongly correlated metal show hints of quantum entanglement in the strange metal state.

Meigan Aronson

Nature Physics

item.title: Nanodiamonds made from tiny graphene triangles
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
Nanodiamonds can host atom-sized light emitters for quantum sensing and imaging, but making nanodiamonds that are small, crystalline and uniform has been difficult. A single-step process for making nanodiamonds only 3–4 nanometres in size uses planar carbon ‘nanographene’ molecules with hydrogen atoms on the edges, and can be adapted to generate fluorescent nanodiamonds.

Nature

item.title: Backreaction of stimulated Hawking radiation in an optical analogue
item.author: Lorenzo M. ProcopioRaul Aguero-SantacruzUlf Leonhardt
item.content:encoded:
By using a fibre-optical analogue of the physics of black holes, theoretical and experimental evidence is presented for the process underpinning the generation of Hawking radiation and its backreaction onto the optical pump.

Lorenzo M. ProcopioRaul Aguero-SantacruzUlf Leonhardt

Nature

item.title: Tin perovskite transistors stabilized through volatile coordination
item.author: Geonwoong ParkDong Hyeon LeeYong-Young Noh
item.content:encoded:
A volatile-assisted coordination strategy regulates surface defect chemistry and self-doping, thus stabilizing the stoichiometry of tin perovskite semiconductors, resulting in transistors that can maintain stable operation for a month at temperatures of 100 °C.

Geonwoong ParkDong Hyeon LeeYong-Young Noh

Nature

item.title: Exchange-mediated spin–electric control of single molecules on surfaces
item.author: Paul GreuleWantong HuangPhilip Willke
item.content:encoded:
Single molecules that host isolated spins are promising candidates for applications in quantum technologies. Now it is shown that exchange interactions enable electric control of spins in two different molecular species.

Paul GreuleWantong HuangPhilip Willke

Nature Physics

item.title: Trump has big AI and quantum ambitions: this scientist’s job is to make them reality
item.author: Elizabeth Gibney
item.content:encoded:
Darío Gil knows that researchers are sceptical of the massive US artificial-intelligence push — he’s asking them to keep an open mind.

Elizabeth Gibney

Nature

item.title: An integrated theoretical and numerical approach to understand modern experiments on quantum magnetism
item.author: Zi Yang MengCristian D. BatistaShiliang Li
item.content:encoded:
Models of quantum magnets have predicted many exciting phenomena that are yet to be observed. This Perspective argues that much tighter collaboration between analytical theory, numerics and experiments is needed to make progress.

Zi Yang MengCristian D. BatistaShiliang Li

Nature Physics

item.title: Coherent control of interacting solid-state spins below the diffraction limit
item.author: Haitong XuMehmet T. UysalJeff D. Thompson
item.content:encoded:
Interacting electron spins in solid-state devices could be used for quantum networks, but such spins are often too closely spaced to be individually addressed. Single-spin control has now been demonstrated for interacting rare-earth dopants.

Haitong XuMehmet T. UysalJeff D. Thompson

Nature Physics

item.title: Ultrashort light pulses can transform a semiconductor into a topological insulator
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
The electronic structure of materials can be tailored using pulses of light, and through this so-called Floquet engineering, novel materials can be designed. Now, the technique has been used to create light-induced metallic surface states in the band gap of a semiconductor — transforming the material, transiently, into a topological insulator.

Nature Physics

item.title: Floquet topological state induced by light-driven band inversion in SnTe
item.author: F. ChassotA. PulkkinenC. Monney
item.content:encoded:
Floquet engineering could, in principle, induce a topological phase in semiconductors, but clear evidence has been lacking. Now it is shown in SnTe that femtosecond light pulses briefly create a topological state that is absent at equilibrium.

F. ChassotA. PulkkinenC. Monney

Nature Physics

item.title: Deuterated compounds for organic light-emitting diodes
item.author: Ross D. Jansen-van VuurenJingwen YaoShou-Cheng Dong
item.content:encoded:
This Review surveys recent advances in deuterated compounds for light-emitting devices, addressing the advantages of deuteration, its working mechanisms, and prospects for wider implementation.

Ross D. Jansen-van VuurenJingwen YaoShou-Cheng Dong

Nature Photonics

item.title: Fourier pixels for bidirectional light control
item.author: Yannik M. GlauserSander J. W. VonkDavid J. Norris
item.content:encoded:
A versatile platform of miniaturized Fourier-optics-based diffractive elements enables multifunctional pixels that fully control and sense the amplitude, phase and polarization of optical wavefronts for advanced photonic applications.

Yannik M. GlauserSander J. W. VonkDavid J. Norris

Nature

item.title: Chiral laser gyroscopes breaking the lock-in limit
item.author: Yuan-Hao MaoJi-Peng XuHui Jing
item.content:encoded:
Ring laser gyroscope lock-in has been eliminated using spontaneous symmetry breaking in a He–Ne laser, enabling accurate near-zero rotation sensing without external components, improving miniaturization and precision.

Yuan-Hao MaoJi-Peng XuHui Jing

Nature

item.title: Optical cooling by interfacial charge transfer in 2D heterostructures
item.author: Jiamin LinBaixu XiangWeigao Xu
item.content:encoded:
Optical cooling in two-dimensional semiconductor heterostructures is demonstrated through phonon-assisted interfacial charge transfer, enabling cryogen-free thermal management without stringent quantum-efficiency requirements.

Jiamin LinBaixu XiangWeigao Xu

Nature

item.title: Unconditional advantage of quantum teleportation over direct transmission of single photons through a lossy channel
item.author: Li-Chao PengDian WuJian-Wei Pan
item.content:encoded:
Shared quantum entanglement can be used to transfer quantum states from one location to another. An experiment has now demonstrated this transfer with higher efficiency than directly sending single photons through a lossy channel.

Li-Chao PengDian WuJian-Wei Pan

Nature Physics

item.title: Emergent partons in fractional quantum Hall systems
item.author: Zihao YangYifan WangLingjie Du
item.content:encoded:
In a geometrical theory for the fractional quantum Hall effect, chiral spin-2 neutral excitations arise. Now, multiple such excitations are observed and provide evidence for the parton description of the quantum Hall effect.

Zihao YangYifan WangLingjie Du

Nature Physics

item.title: A photonic integrated long-distance quantum communication network
item.author: Likang ZhangJiawei PanJian-Wei Pan
item.content:encoded:
A four-user quantum spine-leaf network over up to 540-km fibre is demonstrated using a twin-field quantum key distribution protocol. It allows the full connections between any two user pairs and is operated at a 1.25 GHz clock rate with a secret key rate of 9.38 × 10−9 bit per pulse.

Likang ZhangJiawei PanJian-Wei Pan

Nature Photonics

item.title: A 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum computer with all-to-all connectivity
item.author: Anthony RansfordM. S. AllmanJustin G. Bohnet
item.content:encoded:
A new quantum computer, Quantinuum Helios, which is a 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum processor built on the QCCD architecture, demonstrates performance well beyond classical capabilities and provides a path for scaling up quantum computing.

Anthony RansfordM. S. AllmanJustin G. Bohnet

Nature

item.title: Reconfigurable quantum computer juggles 98 qubits
item.author: Crystal Noel
item.content:encoded:
A quantum computer based on trapped ions can connect any two quantum bits, reaching performance levels that conventional computers cannot match.

Crystal Noel

Nature

item.title: Spatial distribution of the proteome in the human body and in cancers
item.author: Liang YueWenhao JiangTiannan Guo
item.content:encoded:
A spatially resolved map of the human proteome across a variety of healthy tissues and cancers provides wide-ranging insights in developmental biology and oncology, and could aid the identification of therapeutic targets and development of treatments for cancer.

Liang YueWenhao JiangTiannan Guo

Nature

item.title: A prototype differential atom interferometer for fundamental physics
item.author: C. F. A. BaynhamR. HobsonM. G. D. van der Grinten
item.content:encoded:
A prototype differential atom interferometer operates at the standard quantum limit with no excess noise beyond atom shot noise, achieving performance in line with the specifications for future long-baseline atom interferometers.

C. F. A. BaynhamR. HobsonM. G. D. van der Grinten

Nature

item.title: Visualizing the impact of quenched disorder on 2D electron Wigner solids
item.author: Zhehao GeConor SmithMichael F. Crommie
item.content:encoded:
A technique combining atomically resolved scanning tunnelling microscopy with neural-quantum-state quantum Monte Carlo simulation of disordered 2D electron Wigner solids establishes a powerful framework to enable the clear identification of two distinct defect-induced disorder regimes.

Zhehao GeConor SmithMichael F. Crommie

Nature

item.title: Bigger and faster computation with photons
item.author: Lishu Wu
item.content:encoded:
Lishu Wu

Nature Physics

item.title: Strong coupling of a microwave photon to an electron on helium
item.author: Gerwin KoolstraElena O. GlenJohannes Pollanen
item.content:encoded:
Individual electrons on superfluid helium can be used as qubits. Strong coupling between the motional state of a single superfluid helium-bound electron and a single cavity photon has now been demonstrated, enabling qubit readout schemes.

Gerwin KoolstraElena O. GlenJohannes Pollanen

Nature Physics

item.title: Quantum Fisher information in a strange metal
item.author: Federico MazzaSounak BiswasSilke Paschen
item.content:encoded:
A strange metal is a strongly correlated quantum state that occurs in many different materials. Now, quantum Fisher information is shown to increase as the strange metal forms, indicating that the state has high multipartite entanglement.

Federico MazzaSounak BiswasSilke Paschen

Nature Physics

item.title: Tiny thermometers
item.author: Leonardo Benini
item.content:encoded:
Leonardo Benini

Nature Physics

item.title: A productive puzzle
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
High-temperature superconductivity continues to be a crucial research topic in condensed matter physics. We consider the advances that it has inspired.

Nature Physics

item.title: Orbital Hall conductivity and relaxation in thin films with variable disorder
item.author: Min-Gu KangFederica NasrPietro Gambardella
item.content:encoded:
The role of disorder in orbital angular momentum transport induced by electric fields is not well understood. Now orbital Hall conductivity and orbital relaxation are shown to be robust even in strongly disordered thin films.

Min-Gu KangFederica NasrPietro Gambardella

Nature Physics
